summary.brief: Bigler-Engler v. Breg, Inc.. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: The Polar Care device itself had a label with the following warning: "WARNING: A LICENSED HEALTH CARE PRACTITIONER MUST DETERMINE THE CORRECT TEMPERATURE RANGE FOR EACH PATIENT."
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Testing conducted by one of Engler's medical experts revealed that the Polar Care device's temperature controls were ineffective and inadequate.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We conclude the jury's verdict against Breg on Engler's claim for intentional concealment must be reversed.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The jury answered each of the following questions in the affirmative as to Breg: (1) "Did [Breg] intentionally fail to disclose an important fact that Whitney Engler did not know and could not reasonably have discovered?"
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We believe Stern's comments in his opening and closing statements that other evidence of defendants' liability existed but he was prevented from offering it at trial to be the most serious example of counsel's disrespect for the courts.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.chief_complaint: Knee pain and weakness, especially during sports
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient injured her knee while running hurdles, experiencing pain and weakness. Diagnosed with patella alta and possible meniscus tear.

focused_exam.knee: Presence of a loose body underneath the patella

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Knee injury with suspected patella alta and possible meniscus tear

requestables.name: Orthopedic consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr. Chao regarding knee injury

reveal.result_summary: Diagnosis of patella alta and possible meniscus tear
reveal.decision_impact: Led to recommendation for surgery and use of Polar Care device

requestables.name: Surgical consent
requestables.description: Discussion of risks and benefits of knee surgery

reveal.result_summary: Written disclosure of surgical risks provided
reveal.decision_impact: Patient and family agreed to proceed with surgery

key_events.event: Use of Polar Care device as instructed

decision_points.prompt: Should continuous use of the Polar Care device be recommended?

options.description: Recommend intermittent use of the Polar Care device with monitoring
options.clinical_reasoning: Continuous use without monitoring can lead to NFCI; intermittent use is safer

options.description: Recommend continuous use of the Polar Care device as much as possible
options.clinical_reasoning: Believed to decrease risk of infection and aid recovery

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant recommended continuous use of the Polar Care device
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed it was superior to traditional methods

expected_action_court.description: Intermittent use with monitoring should have been recommended
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated continuous use without monitoring was below standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Continuous use led to NFCI; expert testimony indicated need for monitoring
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered cold-related injury due to ineffective temperature control

key_findings: Breg's failure to update device labeling and ensure effective temperature control
Causation established - device's ineffective temperature control led to injury

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to monitor and control device temperature
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper monitoring would have prevented injury

factual_timeline.event: Use of Polar Care device post knee surgery

allegations.allegation: Failure to warn about risks of continuous use
Failure to monitor and control device temperature

court_findings.finding: Breg's failure to update device labeling and ensure effective temperature control
court_findings.legal_significance: Breach of duty leading to injury
